what_it_does: Acceptance script for the orienteering terrain ledger: it gates that every ledger entry path exists on disk, checks the orienteering signoff passes, runs a positive-only orbit-coverage candidate, verifies scan produces a burn_down result, and verifies format_scan renders a readable report.
observations: Imports path_existence, orbit_coverage, orienteering_signoff, scan, format_scan, entry_paths from runtime.orienteering_drift.
Defines a global PASS counter and a check(label, cond) helper that asserts, increments PASS, and prints ok.
The path-existence check is the hard gate and fails loudly if any ledger entry path is missing.
orbit_coverage is checked only to run and return list fields; it is explicitly positive-only and never auto-fails.
scan must return a dict containing a burn_down dict with an open key.
format_scan must return a string containing 'ORIENTEERING DRIFT'.
Uses sys.path insertion to make the project root importable.
standouts: The orbit-coverage check is deliberately positive-only — it runs but never fails the gate, leaving 'is-it-company' judgment to a human.
The path-existence check is the single exact, gate-able assertion.
The check helper combines assertion, counting, and console output in one function.
conventions: Named as an *_acceptance.py file for suite_health auto-discovery.
Uses plain assert with a descriptive failure label.
Docstring explains design rationale and gate semantics.
concerns: Global mutable state PASS is incremented inside check, so a failing assertion aborts the script before the final count is printed.
The positive-only orbit-coverage check only verifies return types, not semantic correctness.
No test framework; plain script with sys.path manipulation.
notes: A self-describing acceptance gate that enforces ledger path existence while keeping orbit coverage as a non-gating, human-reviewed candidate.
questions: What schema do ledger entries follow and what does entry_paths extract?
What does orienteering_signoff evaluate and what can appear in reasons?
What fields does the burn_down model contain beyond open?
How does suite_health discover and run *_acceptance.py files?
Who is 'Tim' and how is the human judgment recorded?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file gates ledger path existence, runs positive-only coverage, and renders a drift read, exactly as the docstring states.
intention_signals: Keep the terrain ledger self-maintaining by preventing silent path rot, treating coverage classification as advisory, and producing a human-readable drift report.
novelty: A positive-only coverage candidate that is explicitly wired to never fail the gate, separating automated path-existence enforcement from human orbit classification.
contribution: An acceptance gate that enforces ledger path existence and exercises the drift scan/render pipeline without auto-failing on coverage classification.
summary_for_embedding: An acceptance test script that gates orienteering ledger path existence, validates signoff, runs positive-only orbit coverage, and checks that scan and format_scan produce a burn_down report.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a fail-loud but coverage-tolerant acceptance gate for the orienteering terrain ledger.

symbols.does: Assertion-and-counter helper that fails the test if cond is false, otherwise increments PASS and prints ok.
symbols.description: The check function is the local test primitive: it asserts the supplied condition with a labeled message, increments a global PASS counter on success, and emits an 'ok' line. All acceptance assertions in the file are expressed through this single helper.
